items.title: Death of Ayatollah Khamenei
items.why_it_matters: Iran has initiated a week-long series of funeral processions for former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei following his death in a February military strike. While state-linked reports focus on the diplomatic attendance and logistical scheduling of the rites, international coverage highlights the broader context of the recent ceasefire between Iran and the United States. The proceedings serve as a focal point for national mourning amidst a period of delicate regional de-escalation.

items.title: Trump and SCOTUS legal developments
items.why_it_matters: The Supreme Court has issued a ruling maintaining birthright citizenship for children born on U.S. soil, interpreting the 14th Amendment as applicable regardless of parental immigration status. This decision has polarized lawmakers, with Republican critics calling for legislative intervention to curb birth tourism and Democratic supporters framing it as a constitutional protection. The reporting highlights the ongoing legislative and judicial friction surrounding immigration policy and the scope of executive authority.

items.title: Death of Iran Supreme Leader
items.why_it_matters: Iran has commenced a seven-day funeral procession for the late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, culminating in a July 9 burial in Mashhad. Reports indicate the delay of these proceedings is linked to ongoing regional hostilities involving the US and Israel. items.title: WHO disease outbreak updates
items.why_it_matters: The World Health Organization has officially ended its surveillance of the hantavirus outbreak associated with the MV Hondius cruise ship after the final contact tested negative. The rare Andes strain caused 13 infections and 3 fatalities following the vessel's April 1 departure from Argentina. Health authorities report no new cases since May 25, marking the successful conclusion of the containment protocol.
